title: Strobilanthes pavala
intro: Strobilanthes pavala, commonly known as "Kalmegh" or "Kutki," is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, indigestion, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes pavala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions, including liver and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Strobilanthes pavala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This family comprises plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny-leaved plants. The Strobilanthes pavala is further classified under the order Lamiales, which includes plants such as lavender, mint, and snapdragons. Within the Acanthaceae family, Strobilanthes pavala is placed in the tribe Rupestres. The species is also known by its synonyms, such as Isostephyr pavala.
